preamble: Reduction of [ε] to the neutral vowel schwa [ə] is an extremely common process in unstressed syllables across many languages, particularly English.

examples.note: The definite article reduces its vowel to schwa when unstressed, a ubiquitous English reduction.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
